rationale: The citing paper uses He, Inman, and Mittal (2008) in its hypothesis-development text to support a specific premise: women prioritize loss avoidance over gain seeking. That cited finding is directly tied to the argument for H1, where the authors predict female CMOs will make less risky marketing decisions. This is more than a passing literature-list mention, even though the citing paper does not directly reuse the cited paper's full model or design.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on He et al. (2008) as empirical support for gendered loss-avoidance tendencies and uses that support to motivate its prediction that female CMOs take less risky actions. The cited work informs the conceptual framing of the gender-risk hypothesis in the new executive decision context.
